summary: Reusable durable row for long-running, deferred, or throttled work that must stay visible beyond a toast. The row keeps subject identity, current job state, freshness or backlog truth, and the next safe action visible in the activity center or other durable shells.

hook_records.notes: Reference shell row for activity center review.
Durable rows must remain parity-quality on light theme.
The row participates in the shared appearance-session contract.
Durable activity rows inherit the same live theme and contrast changes as shell chrome.

slots.description: Focus-owning durable row container in the activity center or equivalent durable surface.
Current job-state icon or semantic activity marker.
Primary identity of the work item.
Current target, provider, workspace, or destination summary.
Current progress, duration, or queued workload summary.
Staleness, deferral, throttling, or backlog note that changes the truth of the row.
Route to logs, evidence, diagnostics, or the owning object.
Safe primary follow-up such as open details, retry, or resume.
Additional action such as pause, cancel, or view logs when the state allows it.

responsive_reflow_notes: Progress and backlog notes collapse before job identity or the next safe action.
In compact layouts the evidence link may move below the action row, but the row itself stays directly focusable.
The row may not demote into a toast-only summary when the work is still durable or recoverable.

state_nodes.user_visible_summary: Work is scheduled but not yet actively running.
Work is actively running and should expose progress or the last truthful checkpoint.
The row needs an explicit user decision before work can continue.
The durable work completed and now links to its resulting object or evidence.
The work failed and the row must name the failure class and next safe action.
The work stopped intentionally or by policy and remains inspectable.
The row shows the last truthful checkpoint while work is deferred, throttled, or partially paused.

transitions.user_visible_effect: Progress and live state appear without changing the row's durable identity.
The row pauses at the current checkpoint and moves the next safe action into the action cluster.
Success state replaces live progress and keeps evidence or result links visible.
The row surfaces the failure class inline and exposes retry or inspect routes.
The row remains durable and names the deferred or throttled reason instead of disappearing.
The row returns to active progress while preserving the prior deferred checkpoint in evidence links.
The row stays visible with the cancellation reason and any retained evidence path.

slot_content_rules.notes: Job identity remains stable across state changes.
Scope summary keeps provider, destination, or workspace identity visible.
Progress speaks in job terms such as queued bytes, passed checks, or current stage.
Deferred, throttled, or stale states remain inline and durable.
Primary follow-up names the action exactly, such as Retry, Resume, or Open details.
Secondary actions stay distinct from the primary next step.
Links to logs, diagnostics, or the owning durable object never replace the primary action.

content_contract.blocked_and_degraded_copy_rule: Deferred, throttled, stale, and failure states must say what still works, what paused, and whether the row represents live work, a queued action, or the last truthful checkpoint.
content_contract.empty_or_missing_data_rule: If exact progress is unavailable, the row still states the current state and the last known truthful checkpoint instead of showing empty chrome.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the next durable row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains inside the durable activity list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the previous durable row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains inside the durable activity list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move from the row surface to row-local actions and evidence links.
primary_navigation_keys.when: Only after the row itself owns focus.

activation_keys.effect: Open the durable row's primary detail or follow-up route.
activation_keys.when: When the row surface owns focus.

activation_keys.effect: Trigger the primary follow-up action when the host maps one for the current state.
activation_keys.when: When the primary action owns focus.

interaction_contract.focus_return_rule: Closing a row-owned detail sheet returns focus to the originating durable row or the nearest surviving durable ancestor.
interaction_contract.pointer_behavior_note: Pointer hover may increase emphasis but must not be required to reveal backlog, throttling, or retry information.

accessibility_contract.role_description: Durable activity list item with optional embedded actions.
accessibility_contract.accessible_name_rule: The accessible name combines the job title and current local state label so users hear both identity and state immediately.
accessibility_contract.accessible_description_rule: The accessible description appends scope summary, progress or backlog note, and the primary next action in stable order.

announcement_rules.message_rule: The row announces meaningful state transitions such as queued to running, running to failed, or running to deferred, but does not spam on every progress tick.
Awaiting-input state announces the next required action and keeps focus on the row until the user moves into the action cluster.
Evidence links speak as links to logs, diagnostics, or the source object rather than as generic details buttons.

accessibility_contract.high_contrast_behavior: State icon, row outline, backlog note, and primary action remain distinct in high-contrast themes without relying on filled progress bars alone.
accessibility_contract.forced_colors_behavior: Forced-colors mode keeps job-state meaning through text, icon, and border emphasis while suppressing decorative fills or subtle progress gradients.
accessibility_contract.blocked_and_degraded_behavior: Deferred or stale rows remain addressable, narrate what is paused or narrowed, and keep retry or inspect routes available from the same durable row.

semantic_token_bindings.notes: Queued rows retain durable visibility without overclaiming progress.
Running rows emphasize truthful progress over ornament.
Deferred or stale notes must stand out in-place.
Follow-up actions stay discoverable in success and failure states.

density_variant_bindings.layout_effect: Compact rows preserve a single-line title and progress summary before lower-priority notes wrap.
Extra breathing room supports longer localized backlog or failure explanations without changing row order.

motion_bindings.reduced_motion_behavior: Progress animation collapses to instant textual updates; no shimmer or pulse survives when motion is reduced or power constrained.

state_visualization_bindings.notes: Queued rows must still name what is waiting.
Action-required state surfaces the exact next step.
Success is durable and links to the resulting object or evidence.
Deferred or throttled rows remain honest about what paused and what still works.

asset_slots.notes: Semantic job-state icon shared across durable job rows.
Progress is primarily textual and numeric; decorative progress imagery is optional.
Evidence and diagnostics links may use a directional affordance.

localization_contract.text_expansion_strategy: The job title truncates last. Backlog and throttling notes may wrap to a second line before actions collapse.
localization_contract.bidi_behavior: Mixed-direction target names or artifact identifiers inside scope summaries remain isolated while surrounding activity copy follows the active locale direction.
localization_contract.formatting_behavior: Durations, counts, and exact times use locale-aware formatting; when a deadline matters the row prefers the exact date or time over vague relative phrasing.
localization_contract.source_language_fallback_behavior: If a provider contributes only a source-language stage label, the row keeps the original label and adds localized framing instead of inventing parity.
localization_contract.notes: Durable rows must remain readable under pseudoloc expansion and retain truthful quantity labels such as blocked count or queued bytes.

allowed_extension_variations: An extension-contributed durable job may add one provider badge or artifact-count fragment inside the scope summary.
An extension-contributed job may omit the secondary action when there is only one safe next step.
extension_guidance.required_gap_disclosure: Extensions that cannot honor durable-row semantics, throttling truth, or host-owned retry language must hand off to the host activity center instead of presenting a toast-only approximation.

substitution_and_deprecation.unsupported_package_behavior: If a theme or provider package cannot express the required state tokens or backlog note treatment, the host durable row falls back to first-party semantic roles and keeps the compatibility gap visible in diagnostics or appearance review.
